plan.failureSummary: The test failed while running in DETERMINISTIC mode because it could not resolve the element for 'Select your occupation'. The cached selector relied on a dynamic ID (#\:rl\:-form-item) which appears to have changed.
observedIndicators: Dynamic ID pattern in failing selector (#\:rl\:-form-item)
Flexible semantic intent ('Fill out all survey questions')

remediationSteps.details: Clear the page.ai cache and re-run the test. This will force the autonomous agent to re-examine the page and find the 'Select your occupation' element using its semantic understanding rather than the broken cached selector.

If the survey consistently changes IDs, consider using a more robust prompt or checking if the 'unstable-survey' app has a 'stable' mode for testing. However, clearing cache is the standard recovery for deterministic selector drift.

plan.notes: The application 'unstable-survey' likely generates dynamic attributes that break deterministic replays. Since the 'overallObjective' is semantically flexible (filling out a form), clearing the AI cache will allow the agent to re-identify the elements based on their labels/roles rather than stale IDs.
